name: Salsa Di Pomodoro a Crudo Di Flavia
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb fresh, sweet, mature vineripened tomatoes or sweet, ripe cherry tomatoes
4 large fresh basil leaves, torn into very small pieces
Freshly milled black pepper to taste
1 large ripe (but not spotted) Hass avocado
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut out the tough area around the core of each tomato and cut the tomatoes into quarters lengthwise.
2. Using your fingers, push out excess seeds. Cut the tomatoes into rough dice or chop them coarsely.
3. Place the tomatoes in the bowl in which you will serve the pasta or in a sauce dish if using with fish.
4. Add the basil, garlic, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
5. Insert a knife into the avocado at the top, where the navel is.
6. Cut into the avocado until you reach the seed, then make a clean incision all the way around the length of the fruit.
7. Grasp one half in each hand and twist the halves in opposite directions to open the avocado. The seed should fall right out.
8. Peel and thinly slice each half crosswise; then cut the slices further into small strips or dice.
9. Add the avocado to the bowl with the other ingredients.
10. Toss to mix and allow to stand at room temperature while the pasta or fish cooks.
Makes approximately 2 1/2 cups
Note: This recipe is sufficient for saucing 1 pound of pasta.
Ahead of time note: Make the sauce up to 2 hours in advance. Cover and leave at room temperature until ready to serve.
